actual_zns_overhead.caveat: Actual-ZNS latency is measured through zonefs helper appends/truncates and includes user-space helper overhead. C-level CPU numbers isolate only placement-decision cost.
actual_zns_overhead.scope: actual ZNS replay overhead plus C-level policy-decision overhead

adaptive_policy_comparison.decision_reason: Adaptive binning did not beat the current hybrid on WAF, GC blocks, stale-secret blocks, or family count in the current single-tenant pressure suite.
adaptive_policy_comparison.scope: adaptive QUASAR admission/binning comparison on YCSB and Sysbench pressure suites

claims.caveat: This is a semantic/exposure claim; WAF may remain near 1.0 on easy traces.
claims.claim: Storage-history baselines miss PQC death cohorts.
evidence: same-path physical ZNS fairness rows=72 failed=0
claims.paper_wording: FIFO, SepBIT-style, MiDAS-style, and DOGI-style placement retain expired PQC secret bytes because they do not receive protocol death-cohort information.

claims.caveat: Requires correct lifecycle hints and durable epoch-close logic.
claims.claim: QUASAR-DOGI hybrid removes stale-secret exposure on clean hinted actual-ZNS replays.
evidence: same-path physical ZNS hybrid secret waiting end is zero
claims.paper_wording: QUASAR-DOGI hybrid keeps DOGI-style payload placement while placing PQC secrets by death cohort, making expired secret cohorts reset-eligible.

claims.caveat: Do not claim QUASAR dominates every workload on total WAF.
claims.claim: WAF/GC gains are pressure-dependent, not universal.
claims.paper_wording: WAF improvements appear when PQC metadata density and free-zone pressure create real GC work; otherwise the main benefit is stale-secret exposure reduction.

claims.caveat: Clean epoch traces remain sanity checks; the main benchmark must use DOGI/FAST-compatible overlays and hostile stress.
claims.claim: The evaluation is not based on an overly easy PQC-only trace.
claims.paper_wording: The workload suite separates negative controls, DOGI-compatible pressure workloads, FAST-style DB pressure, and QUASAR-hostile robustness cases.

claims.caveat: Do not present adaptive binning or strict residual migration as free default behavior.
claims.claim: The improved deployable QUASAR design uses explicit modes rather than one universal knob.
claims.paper_wording: QUASAR-DOGI hybrid is the default; tenant isolation, residual migration, and overflow fallback are enabled only when the measured workload/security objective requires their overhead.

claims.caveat: The manifest indexes current artifacts; long-running raw physical replays may still need explicit rerun time and device availability.
claims.claim: The actual-ZNS comparison is reproducible from an artifact manifest.
claims.paper_wording: The paper artifact set records hashes, roles, claims, and regeneration commands for the actual-ZNS comparison, pressure curves, overhead, selector, figures, and acceptance reports; a validation pass confirms the current files still match those hashes.

claims.caveat: Strict mode is not the default for every workload.
claims.claim: Residual migration is a deployable strict-exposure mode with explicit cost.
claims.paper_wording: QUASAR exposes low-overhead, balanced, and strict-zero-wait modes; strict exposure can force zero waiting but may pay high copy/WAF cost on hostile YCSB-F straggler workloads.

claims.caveat: Actual-ZNS latency uses zonefs helper appends/truncates, so use as overhead accounting, not final production p99.
claims.claim: Hybrid has explicit reset overhead but lower policy-decision CPU cost than DOGI-style MLP.
claims.paper_wording: Hybrid pays actual semantic reset work but its C-level placement-decision path remains cheaper than DOGI-style MLP inference.

claims.caveat: QUASAR proves reset eligibility and stale-secret exposure reduction, and this device's NVMe crypto-erase sanitize command path has been executed and validated as a destructive device/namespace-scoped operation. Zone reset alone is still not a physical erase proof, and sanitize must not be treated as a per-zone or per-epoch command on a shared namespace. A strong physical erase deployment requires a dedicated namespace/media pool, per-cohort encryption-key isolation, or future per-zone erase semantics whose blast radius matches the cohort being destroyed.
claims.claim: Zone reset alone is not physical erase; sanitize is validated only as a destructive device/namespace-scoped path.
evidence: sanitize_log_status=(1) Most Recent Sanitize Command Completed Successfully.
claims.paper_wording: QUASAR proves reset eligibility and stale-secret exposure reduction. On the evaluated device, NVMe crypto-erase sanitize completed successfully as a destructive device/namespace-scoped operation. It validates command support, not per-zone physical erase. Physical-erasure claims require dedicated namespaces/media pools, per-cohort key isolation, or hardware semantics whose blast radius matches the cohort.

claims.caveat: Do not mix exact-baseline internal units with QUASAR native ZNS throughput as if they were identical.
claims.claim: Exact external baselines are included but have non-identical unit systems.
claims.paper_wording: External DOGI/MiDAS/SepBIT runs validate baseline realism, while same-path packed ZNS replay is used for apples-to-apples physical placement comparison.

claims.caveat: This is a trace-driven handle-pressure model, not a physical FDP device performance result.
claims.claim: FDP can carry QUASAR's lifecycle signal, but scarce placement handles create collision pressure.
claims.paper_wording: A trace-driven FDP mapping shows that QUASAR families retain high intent/family purity as placement handles increase, but small handle counts collide multiple death cohorts and require admission or binning.

claims.caveat: Lower-overhead xNVMe/SPDK replay remains optional strengthening.
claims.claim: The current artifact set is paper-ready for the scoped system claim.
claims.paper_wording: The scoped claim is ready: PQC death-cohort hints improve ZNS placement/reset scheduling under clean hints, pressure, and measured fallback modes.

deployment_selector.main_takeaway: The improved deployable design is not a single universal knob. The default remains QUASAR-DOGI hybrid; tenant isolation and residual migration are explicit modes enabled only when the workload/security objective requires their overhead.

modes.rule: Use exact PQC death-cohort placement for secrets and DOGI-style fallback for payload.
modes.when: single-tenant or normal PQC pressure without tenant isolation requirement

modes.rule: Add tenant-local death-cohort bins only when reset-time tenant impurity is a security/isolation concern.
modes.when: multi-tenant secret reset must not mix tenant cohorts

modes.rule: Select low-overhead, balanced, or strict-zero-wait residual budget based on exposure objective.
modes.when: delayed expiry or stragglers would otherwise leave secret bytes waiting

modes.rule: Route uncertain data away from exact secret zones and reset only after epoch-manager confirmation.
modes.when: hint confidence is low, missing, or inconsistent

deployment_selector.scope: QUASAR deployment policy selector derived from actual-ZNS and simulator pressure artifacts

selector.condition: strict zero stale-secret waiting required under stragglers
hint confidence is low or missing

dogi_physical_compact.caveat: Compacted LBA span preserves reuse order but is not the full original LBA span used by a production run.
dogi_physical_compact.scope: exact external DOGI prototype on physical ZNS, compacted LBA span

dogi_physical_dynamic_pressure.caveat: This is the public DOGI binary on a compacted Alibaba-like PQC pressure trace. It validates the exact DOGI stack on the physical ZNS device, but units still differ from QUASAR's same-path packed replay.
dogi_physical_dynamic_pressure.scope: exact external DOGI prototype on physical ZNS, Alibaba-like p8000 compact trace

dogi_physical_dynamic_pressure_suite.caveat: These are public DOGI prototype internal GiB counters on a compacted trace. They are exact DOGI-stack evidence but should not be mixed as apples-to-apples numbers with QUASAR's packed replay metrics.
dogi_physical_dynamic_pressure_suite.scope: exact public DOGI prototype placement variants on physical ZNS, Alibaba-like p8000 compact trace

dogi_physical_original_lba_pressure.caveat: This run keeps the original LBA span and therefore removes the compact-LBA caveat for the Alibaba-like p8000 DOGI pressure trace. It is still an exact DOGI-stack counter, not a same-path QUASAR packed-replay metric.
dogi_physical_original_lba_pressure.scope: exact external DOGI prototype on physical ZNS, Alibaba-like p8000 original LBA span

midas_memory_repeat4.caveat: MiDAS exact artifact uses internal page/traffic units and a memory-backed prototype, so it is evidence about MiDAS strength but not a direct ZNS throughput comparison.
midas_memory_repeat4.scope: exact external MiDAS memory-backed prototype on exchange-pqc2000 repeat4 compact trace

sepbit_repeat4.caveat: SepBIT numbers are trace_replay simulator WA, not native ZNS physical append latency.
sepbit_repeat4.scope: exact external SepBIT trace_replay simulator on the same exchange repeat4 compact trace

fast_db_pressure.scope: FAST-style Sysbench-OLTP pressure stress, not a DOGI paper workload

fast_ycsb_pressure.scope: FAST/YCSB-A/F pressure stress with higher PQC ratios and tight free-zone margins

multitenant_pressure.scope: multi-tenant PQC pressure with reset-time secret tenant isolation

physical_robustness.decision_reason: Exact secret-group packing is best for clean hints, missing hints, and wrong epochs, but delayed expiry/stragglers can exceed the WD ZN540 open-zone budget. Epoch-bin-4 keeps max live physical zones at the device limit and completes, but leaves stale-secret exposure. Epoch-bin-5 plus residual migration completes on the actual ZNS device, keeps max live physical zones within mor/mar, and reduces final secret waiting to zero at explicit GC-copy cost.
physical_robustness.scope: actual ZNS bad-hint and straggler replay on YCSB-A p4000

security_capability.claim_boundary: QUASAR proves reset eligibility and stale-secret exposure reduction, and this device's NVMe crypto-erase sanitize command path has been executed and validated as a destructive device/namespace-scoped operation. Zone reset alone is still not a physical erase proof, and sanitize must not be treated as a per-zone or per-epoch command on a shared namespace. A strong physical erase deployment requires a dedicated namespace/media pool, per-cohort encryption-key isolation, or future per-zone erase semantics whose blast radius matches the cohort being destroyed.
security_capability.sanitize_log_status: (1) Most Recent Sanitize Command Completed Successfully.

entries.name: DOGI six-axis fairness matrix
entries.paper_use: Use for fairness and semantic-exposure evidence; do not oversell WAF here.
entries.purpose: Match DOGI/FAST workload axes without pretending every row is a WAF stress test.

entries.name: DOGI-favorable non-PQC control
entries.paper_use: Use before the PQC-overlay results: QUASAR is not a universal history-placement replacement; the hybrid preserves DOGI's advantage.
entries.purpose: Prove that the evaluation does not cripple DOGI: when no PQC death-cohort signal exists, DOGI-style history placement is the right tool.

entries.name: YCSB p2000 negative WAF control
entries.paper_use: Use as an honest negative control: WAF does not separate, but semantic reset does.
entries.purpose: Prove that easy DOGI-axis workloads are not WAF stress tests.

entries.name: YCSB p4000/p6000/p8000/p10000 DOGI-compatible pressure
entries.paper_use: Use as the main DOGI-compatible WAF/GC pressure curve.
entries.purpose: Use DOGI/FAST YCSB axes with enough PQC density and zone pressure to create GC.

entries.name: Sysbench-OLTP FAST-style DB pressure
entries.paper_use: Label as FAST-style DB pressure, not a DOGI-paper workload.
entries.purpose: Show that the result is not only a synthetic YCSB artifact.

evidence.minimum_rule: >=3 YCSB pressure rows with all history baselines, DB pressure eligible, >=2 dynamic pressure rows with all policies

entries.name: Main WAF/GC claim eligibility gate
entries.paper_use: Use only these eligible pressure rows for the headline WAF/GC claim; keep easy p2000 rows as negative controls.
entries.purpose: Prevent easy negative-control workloads from being used as the headline WAF figure.

entries.name: Multi-tenant PQC pressure
entries.paper_use: Use to justify a tenant-isolation mode rather than one universal allocator setting.
entries.purpose: Attack QUASAR's open-zone/family count assumptions with many tenants.

entries.name: Bad-hint and straggler robustness
entries.paper_use: Use as the design-boundary figure: exact cohorting is not enough under stragglers.
entries.purpose: Show that QUASAR has boundaries and needs fallback under imperfect lifetimes.

entries.name: Residual fallback frontier
entries.paper_use: Use to present low-overhead, balanced, and strict-zero-wait modes.
entries.purpose: Measure when strict zero-wait exposure is affordable and when it is too expensive.

workload_hardness.main_takeaway: The suite now contains a DOGI-favorable non-PQC control, a negative WAF control, DOGI-compatible pressure rows, FAST-style DB pressure, dynamic service pressure, an explicit main-claim eligibility gate, and QUASAR-hostile robustness cases. This prevents the paper from relying on an overly easy PQC-only workload.
workload_hardness.scope: DOGI/FAST-compatible workload hardness matrix for QUASAR evaluation

xnvme_zns_latency.caveat: Raw xNVMe/Linux NVMe ioctl Zone Append probe. This bypasses zonefs helper overhead, but it is not an SPDK poll-mode result because the local build lacks a new enough liburing/SPDK backend.

ycsb_f_straggler_baselines.scope: YCSB-F p8000 with 5% delayed-expiry stragglers on actual ZNS for history baselines
